title: AlphaChip public-record audit

source.snapshot_note: This fixture transcribes the sourced Chapter 8 audit. It is a fixed teaching snapshot, not a live status feed or an independent reassessment of the underlying work.

interpretation_boundary: Field status describes what traveled in the cited public record. Absent or partial does not imply that the original team lacked the corresponding internal process or artifact.

card_fields.public_record_2021: Present. The reported intent was to compress TPU-block macro placement from weeks of expert work to hours at comparable or better quality on key power, performance, and area metrics.
card_fields.public_record_after_2024_addendum: Unchanged. The intent was restated with the AlphaChip name and a deployment claim across TPU generations.

card_fields.public_record_2021: Present. Macro placement on production netlists was a bounded physical-design task.
Partial. The placement action space was described, while the production blocks, constraints, and flow settings that defined legality stayed internal.
card_fields.public_record_after_2024_addendum: Open test cases exist from the reassessment flow, not from the original production flow.

card_fields.public_record_2021: Present. The paper described the netlist-as-graph encoding.
card_fields.public_record_after_2024_addendum: The released code makes the representation inspectable.

card_fields.public_record_2021: Absent from the public packet. A runnable environment, tool contract, and matching test cases did not travel with the claim.
card_fields.public_record_after_2024_addendum: Partially closed. Code and a pretrained checkpoint are public, while the production flow and blocks remain internal.

card_fields.public_record_2021: Present. A reinforcement-learning policy generated placements inside a larger physical-design flow.
Absent from the public packet. The pretraining corpus, compute, and convergence criteria were not stated as a budget another team could match.
card_fields.public_record_after_2024_addendum: Central to the dispute. The rebuttal argued that the critics skipped pretraining, under-trained, and used substantially less compute.

card_fields.public_record_2021: Disputed. Comparisons used proprietary blocks and a proxy cost that the reassessment later reported correlated weakly with post-route metrics.
card_fields.public_record_after_2024_addendum: The reassessment added open evidence on its own cases. The evidence behind the strongest original claims remains outside a public runnable packet.

card_fields.public_record_2021: Absent. Failed or rejected placements, seeds, and excluded cases were not published.
card_fields.public_record_after_2024_addendum: Still absent from the public packets on both sides.

card_fields.public_record_2021: Absent as a shared gate. Both sides had strong private checks, but the public record named no rejection authority accepted by both.
card_fields.public_record_after_2024_addendum: The shared-gate gap remains the relevant public-audit limitation in the Chapter 8 snapshot.

card_fields.public_record_2021: Absent from the public packet. The strongest public claim did not state what evidence would narrow or overturn it.
card_fields.public_record_after_2024_addendum: The journal process closed, while the cited public record still supplied no explicit overturning condition for the strongest claim.

card_fields.public_record_2021: Partial. Deployment decisions were owned inside Google, while the public record did not name the review that accepted them.
